problem_addressed: Reward models in RLHF overlook epistemic uncertainty from limited human feedback, leading to reward overoptimization and expensive annotation costs.
core_claim: Model size and initialization have the most meaningful impact on uncertainty quantification performance, and most prior work could have benefited from alternative design choices.
key_contributions: Unified framework RewardUQ for systematic evaluation of uncertainty quantification methods for reward models
Comparison of common UQ methods along accuracy and calibration metrics with new ranking strategy
Open-source Python package for framework deployment
novelty: First unified framework to systematically compare uncertainty quantification methods for reward models using standardized metrics and datasets
method_summary: RewardUQ trains and evaluates four uncertainty quantification architectures (ENS-MLP, ENS-LoRA, MCD-DPO, BAY-LIN) across different datasets and base models using unified evaluation procedure on RewardBench
method_components: MLP head ensemble (ENS-MLP) with K independent heads
LoRA adapter ensemble (ENS-LoRA) with parameter-efficient fine-tuning
DPO-based MC dropout (MCD-DPO) using dropout before LM head
Bayesian linear head (BAY-LIN) with Gaussian prior and Laplace approximation
key_innovation: Unified evaluation framework enabling systematic comparison of UQ methods across standardized metrics and datasets
assumptions: Epistemic uncertainty arises from limited preference data
Reward and uncertainty can be separated in model architecture
Confidence bounds can be constructed using mean and standard deviation
Cross-entropy loss is appropriate for training reward models
theoretical_grounding: Uses Laplace approximation for Bayesian linear regression and Monte Carlo dropout for uncertainty estimation, but does not provide new theoretical proofs
unstated_weaknesses: Focus on LLM reward models may not directly transfer to medical image classification
Paper focuses on comparison rather than proposing a novel method
Experimental results are cut off, limiting understanding of full findings
threats_to_validity: Generalization from LLM reward models to medical image classification is uncertain
Comparison might be limited to specific types of uncertainty quantification methods
Abstract doesn't mention datasets or benchmarks used for evaluation
open_questions: How do the findings about model size and initialization impact medical image classification?
What specific uncertainty quantification methods were compared?
How does the proposed ranking strategy perform in practice?
future_work: Extending the framework to other domains
Applying the findings to medical image classification
gaps_this_enables: Better uncertainty-aware reward models for medical image classification
Insights into selecting appropriate uncertainty quantification methods
Informing the development of calibration-aware active learning strategies for medical imaging
relevance_to_topic: The paper's focus on uncertainty quantification and calibration is directly relevant to calibration-aware active learning for medical image classification, though its focus on LLM reward models rather than image classification is a limitation.
reusable_assets: Framework for evaluating uncertainty quantification methods
title: RewardUQ: A Unified Framework for Uncertainty-Aware Reward Models
